objective.definition: Default dogfooding is documented, eval-aligned, and completed-run memory has terminalization plus continuation trace contracts.

anti_goals.definition: No semantic memory, completion, or guardrail claim is accepted from one model narrative alone.
Previous-run learning stays candidate-only until current evidence and human ratification justify promotion.
No completed-run learning is reused without retained traces, consolidation output, and continuation packet.

memory_progression_default_dogfood_ready.source: repository diff plus deterministic validation commands
memory_progression_default_dogfood_ready.freshness_rule: command outputs recorded during this run are fresh for the final report

single_llm_truth_acceptance_count.source: deterministic checks plus independent review attempts or review set evidence
single_llm_truth_acceptance_count.freshness_rule: must be read before completion claim

allowed_moves: run deterministic validation and dry-run dispatch
forbidden_moves: commit or push without explicit user request
claim scored model review passed when API-backed review was unavailable
promote prior-run memory as authority without ratification
approval_gates: human owns objective, anti-goals, guardrail thresholds, and action envelope

rejected_guardrails.guardrail: require external scored review before any local artifact can exist
rejected_guardrails.rationale: environment can block model API review; record blocker and keep deterministic evidence instead of hiding the gap

human_approval.source: user requested default dogfooding, eval alignment, full-cycle memory strategy, and anti-goal of no single LLM trust; user then granted permission and resumed implementation
